claim: Safe operator-channel onboarding now has explicit repair-next-step evidence for invalid token, signature mismatch, replay suspicion, and missing heartbeat governance visibility across Hub admin live-test evidence, doctor recovery summaries, and local Hub Swift parity builders, while preserving Hub-first quarantine, approval, revoke, and replay fail-closed boundaries.

verification_results.name: hub_live_test_evidence_promotes_invalid_token_signature_and_replay_repairs
verification_results.detail: operator_channel_live_test_evidence.test now proves invalid-token, signature mismatch, and replay suspicion all surface as explicit required_next_step guidance instead of stopping at lower-level repair-hint generation.

verification_results.name: admin_http_live_test_endpoint_preserves_repair_guidance_end_to_end
verification_results.detail: channel_onboarding_admin_http.test verifies the local admin evidence endpoint returns Hub-built reports whose repair_hints and required_next_step include Slack signature remediation, while preserving onboarding snapshot and release-boundary checks.

verification_results.name: operator_channel_recovery_report_highlights_missing_heartbeat_governance_visibility
verification_results.detail: generate_xhub_operator_channel_recovery_report.test now proves missing first-smoke heartbeat governance visibility is elevated into dedicated recovery guidance and blocked release wording instead of hiding under generic live-test failure phrasing.

verification_results.name: relflowhub_swift_parity_builder_matches_hub_evidence_contract
verification_results.detail: OperatorChannelsOnboardingSupportTests now pass with explicit Swift-side coverage for signature mismatch and replay suspicion, so Hub local UI no longer depends on JS-only correctness for live-test evidence phrasing.

verification_results.name: replay_and_onboarding_regressions_rechecked_after_swift_side_fixes
verification_results.detail: Focused reruns of channel_onboarding_admin_http.test and pairing_http_preauth_replay.test passed after the Hub app-side compile/test fixes, confirming no regression in revoke, replay TTL, or fail-closed onboarding flows.

verification_results.name: dedicated_safe_onboarding_gate_packages_regressions_and_packet_refresh
verification_results.detail: scripts/ci/xt_w3_24_s_safe_onboarding_gate.sh now reruns the focused Node regressions, Swift parity tests, generator regression, and tracked evidence refresh as one repeatable release-facing gate.

verification_results.name: github_actions_workflow_replays_safe_onboarding_gate
verification_results.detail: .github/workflows/xt-w3-24-safe-onboarding-gate.yml now gives XT-W3-24-S a macOS GitHub Actions entrypoint that uploads the gate summary, logs, and tracked evidence packet.

verification_results.name: release_operator_docs_bind_safe_onboarding_packet_to_preview_support_lane
verification_results.detail: OSS release checklist and release-notes templates now explicitly point operators at the XT-W3-24-S gate outputs while keeping that packet in the preview/support lane instead of silently broadening validated public claims.

bounded_gaps.name: first_run_shell_not_fully_polished
bounded_gaps.detail: Safe onboarding is working and test-covered, but the first-run product shell is still not polished enough to upgrade from preview-working to validated.

bounded_gaps.name: whatsapp_cloud_release_wording_still_require_real_bound
bounded_gaps.detail: WhatsApp Cloud API remains under require-real release gating and must not be described as fully ready just because the onboarding and repair path is structurally covered.

bounded_gaps.name: preview_support_scope_must_remain_narrow
bounded_gaps.detail: Even with the focused gate, GitHub Actions workflow, and release-operator docs wired up, XT-W3-24-S remains preview/support evidence and must not be promoted into validated public release claims yet.
